title: carex vulgaris subsp. dematranea
intro: Carex vulgaris subsp. dematranea, also known as the common sedge or wood sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Carex vulgaris subsp. dematranea include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
